capabilities: Combine rate limiting strategies with agent planning insights to optimize request handling and prevent overload.

procedure.action: Reconcile artifacts from rate limiting and agent planning by applying domain-specific tooling; ensure all shared steps are aligned and conflicts are resolved using hash-based comparison by applying field-level structural comparison on shared key fields; flag conflicts where hash(A.field) != hash(B.field) and log to conflict_log; assert merge_error_count < 1.

Assert merged output by running domain-specific validation tools; assert that error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true, logging any discrepancies by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail after each merge operation to maintain a comprehensive record of all transactions and their outcomes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Filter candidates for rate limiting by using domain-specific tooling to analyze request patterns; ensure error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true by applying a predicate filter with threshold score > 0.6 on each candidate record; discard records below threshold and log detection_count; assert false_positive_rate < 0.05.

Assign results from the merged knowledge base using domain-specific tooling; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail for transparency and traceability using a deterministic decision matrix: evaluate each criterion in priority order; assign first matching class or UNCLASSIFIED; assert confidence_score >= 0.7 for all classified records.

summary: Integrate rate limiting with agent planning derived knowledge base by merging workflows and validating outputs to ensure seamless operation.
title: Rate Limiting

failureConditions: Inconsistent data when merge omits NULL handling: records with NULL key fields are silently dropped, causing referential integrity violations in downstream consumers.
Rate limiting fails when agent planning insights are not integrated, causing request overload and potential system downtime.
Audit trail gaps occur when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, leading to incomplete transaction records.
metrics: merged output by running domain-specific validation tools
that error_count is less than 1 and result_valid equals true, logging any discrepancies by asserting all required fields
